title: ForgeBrain Topic Research Brief
description: The structured, lesson-ready output of the research stage for a single curriculum topic. Produced after topic selection (see topic-selector-spec.md) and consumed by the Lesson Generator. See research-spec.md for the full process this schema supports.

topic_id.description: Matches the corresponding id in curriculum/java-roadmap.json.

topic_title.description: Matches the corresponding title in curriculum/java-roadmap.json.

topic_summary.description: One to three plain-language sentences on what this topic is and why it matters. Seed material for the reel's hook.

learning_objective.description: The single outcome the reel must deliver. Mirrors curriculum learning_objective, refined if research surfaced a clearer framing.

difficulty.description: Mirrors the curriculum topic's difficulty tier.

audience_level.description: Audience level this brief was written for. May differ from difficulty if explicitly requested by the caller.

target_reel_length_seconds.description: The reel length this brief was scoped for. Bounds how many core_concepts and code_example_ideas are reasonable.

prerequisites.description: Minimal references to prerequisite topics. The audience can be assumed to already know these; the brief should not re-explain them.

common_misconceptions.description: 2-4 items recommended. What beginners commonly get wrong about this topic. Strong hook and review-checklist material.

core_concepts.description: 3-5 items recommended, in teaching order. The fixed set of ideas the reel must cover.

simple_analogy.description: One clear, concrete analogy for this topic, validated for accuracy. Used in the hook and/or the core explanation.

code_example_ideas.description: 2-4 items recommended. Pre-selected example concepts the Script Generator can choose from and turn into an actual code sample.

beginner_explanation.description: A plain-language explanation of the topic, short enough to read aloud in under 20 seconds, that the Lesson Generator can adapt directly into narration.

advanced_notes.description: Correctness nuances beyond the beginner explanation. Not meant to be spoken in the reel; used by the Reviewer to confirm the simplified explanation didn't become false.

related_topics.description: topic_ids of related topics, mirroring curriculum related_topics. Useful for callbacks or a closing 'next up' line.

safety_notes.description: Explicit statements about this topic that must NOT be said, because they are technically incorrect or misleading. The primary hallucination guardrail, read by the Reviewer stage.

sources.description: Reserved for future citation tracking. Empty in Phase 1, where no external source-fetching pipeline exists yet.

research_version.description: Version of the research process/schema that produced this brief, for future caching and migration.

sourceRef.description: Reserved shape for a future cited source. Not populated in Phase 1.

trust_tier.description: Used by future source-ranking logic to weigh conflicting sources.

overall_confidence.description: Coarse self-assessment of how well-grounded this brief is.

flagged_uncertainties.description: Specific points the research stage wasn't fully certain about, flagged for reviewer attention rather than presented as settled fact.

unresolved_conflicts.description: Reserved for future multi-source contradiction handling. Empty until multiple sources are actually cross-checked against each other.
